msgs.text:
I treated the 42 prior turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt fixes the submission format: sigma,tau are distinguished tame generators with the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves only the dynamic tame-inertia candidate alive; the scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants were refuted there by explicit finite-tame or index-two subgroup tests.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2 and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The external benchmark remains exactly the one emphasized in the prior turns: the Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg method for pÓÄ†=2 works by checking the DemuŇ°kin kernel over every finite normal tame subextension, tracking the finite tame action on units, and then applying a Koch/DemuŇ°kin-formation uniqueness theorem; Neukirch describes this as a DemuŇ°kin-formation argument, not merely an abstract pro-p kernel calculation.Numdam Jarden‚ÄďShusterman also explicitly note that the Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg explicit description is for pÓÄ†=2, that Diekert‚Äôs dyadic analogue does not cover K=Q2, and that the minimal generator and relation counts are [K:Qp]+2 and [K:Qp]+1.Mathematics at TAU The main correction this turn is that the sign-cover coefficient must be fixed once and for all. Several prior turns state the right radical/unit conclusion but display the fraction in inconsistent directions. Let V=kerőļ be the sign double cover, let t be its deck involution, and restrict to a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block where ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient is the completed geometric seriesDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

The identity is (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ.Since ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode) on an e-th tame-character block,(tőĪa)ŌÄ=t.

Therefore the correct formula isDa=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít.

This direction is forced: 1‚ąítőĪa is a unit because őĪaÓÄ†=1, while 1‚ąít lies in the radical of the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra. Thus Da is radical, and the coefficient contributed by the correction factorx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄis‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑,which is a unit. So the intended conclusion survives: every nontrivial odd tame-character block contributes only unit Smith pivots to the sign-kernel abelianization. But the usable formula is (1‚ąít)/(1‚ąítőĪa), not its reciprocal.The trivial tame-inertia elimination has an analogous direction issue. With T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

Write f=2a m, m odd. Then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. Hence the valid substitution isY=Ps (T)X+YňČ,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís up to the convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. The reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1).With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),for a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. The trivial block nonsingularity proof is therefore sound, but only with Ps=(1+T‚ąís)/(1+T).With those two corrected formulas, the previously claimed abelianization checks remain internally consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and, for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4.

The C2 and C4 torsion is entirely in the tame-inertia-invariant block; nontrivial odd tame-character blocks are unit-pivot blocks.My additional refinement is about the first mixed even-f filtered-action target in (e,f)=(3,2). The candidate-side class should be viewed modulo the Frobenius-lift gauge. In the S3 -quotient, putAi=XŌĄi,Bi=XŌĄiŌÉ,i‚ąąZ/3,andZd=i‚ąąZ/3‚ąĎ[Ai,Bi+d].The candidate‚Äôs first mixed class is represented, after choosing the standard lift ŌÉ, by the relation-preserving derivationőīd (b)=Zd,őīd(Ai)=[Bi+d,S],őīd(Bi+d)=[Ai,S],őīd (S)=0.

For d=0, this is the class prior turns called Z0. It preserves the quadratic DemuŇ°kin initial relationq=i‚ąĎ(Ai[2]+Bi[2])+[b,S],because Jacobi gives cancellation of the [Z0,S] term in characteristic 2.However, d=0 is not gauge-invariant. Replacing the tame Frobenius lift byŌÉ‚Ä≤=ŌĄkŌÉ cyclesZd‚Ü¶Zd+k.

So the arithmetic comparison target is not intrinsically ‚ÄúZ0 ‚ÄĚ unless the same Frobenius lift is fixed on the arithmetic side. The gauge-invariant statement is that the class lies in the orbit{őī0,őī1,őī2},and, after choosing the standard arithmetic lift fixing 21/3 in L=Q2(ő∂3,21/3), the expected representative should be őī0.That does not prove the presentation. It sharpens the remaining comparison problem: the live word has survived rank, cup-product, abelianization, sign-kernel, cyclotomic-branch, and first mixed filtered-action consistency checks, but a final proof still needs the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem identifying the exact filtered (ŌÉ,ŌĄ)-operator action with arithmetic over all finite tame subextensions.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I fixed the two recurring reciprocal errors:Da=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís (up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1). With these directions, nontrivial tame-character sign-cover blocks are unit-pivot blocks, and the conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4 survive. I also clarified that the mixed (3,2) class is őī0 only after fixing a Frobenius-lift gauge; intrinsically it is the orbit {őī0,őī1,őī2}.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Fix L=Q2(ő∂3,21/3) and the Frobenius lift fixing 21/3. Compute the arithmetic action on GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5, with target őī0 for that lift, or the orbit {őī0,őī1,őī2} before fixing the lift.[BREAKTHROUGH]
